skill.name: google-maps-reviews-api-skill
skill.description: Extract Google Maps review data through a BrowserAct workflow using keywords, language, and country filters.
skill.summary: Collect Google Maps reviews through a BrowserAct API workflow.
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: Static findings for network access, environment access, and script execution are mostly true in the intended BrowserAct workflow, but I found no evidence of malicious exfiltration, obfuscation, prompt injection, or unauthorized command execution. The critical heuristic is dismissed because the API key is used for the documented BrowserAct endpoint, while the remaining risk is third-party data transmission and local script execution.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party API Requests Use a Bearer Token
medium_findings.description: The script reads BROWSERACT_API_KEY and sends it as an Authorization bearer token to https://api.browseract.com. This is the documented purpose of the skill, but it exposes user search parameters and the token to a third-party API.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network calls and bearer token construction are direct in the script. Confidence is high, but the behavior appears intentional rather than malicious.

medium_findings.title: Skill Requires Local Script Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to run a Python script with user-provided keywords, language, and country arguments. The script does not spawn additional commands, but marketplace users should understand that it executes local code and performs network requests.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The execution path is explicitly documented and the script entry point accepts command-line arguments. I found no evidence of shell command construction or subprocess use inside the script.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Execution Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: Many static external-command detections in SKILL.md are inline Markdown examples for parameter names, supported values, output fields, and one documented Python command. They are not Ruby backtick execution in source code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text appears inside Markdown documentation, not executable Ruby or shell source. The only executable instruction is the intended Python invocation.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto and Reconnaissance Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The flagged SKILL.md lines describe Google Maps review features and input fields, and the script lines print authorization errors or inspect API response text. I found no cryptographic operation, host reconnaissance, or system probing behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited lines found ordinary documentation and response handling. No evidence supports weak cryptography or reconnaissance semantics.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network Plus Credential Access Combination
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill combines environment-variable credential access with outbound HTTPS calls. This is expected for BrowserAct API use, but it is the primary security pattern users should review before publishing.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The combination is directly present in the script. It is risky by category, but I did not find evidence of hidden endpoints or credential misuse.

content.user_title: Extract Google Maps Reviews with BrowserAct
content.value_statement: Manual review collection is slow and hard to repeat across locations. This skill uses a BrowserAct workflow to request Google Maps review data with clear inputs and status logs.
actual_capabilities: Starts a BrowserAct Google Maps Reviews workflow by template ID.
Accepts search keywords, language, and country as command-line inputs.
Reads BROWSERACT_API_KEY from the environment and reports missing or invalid keys.
Polls BrowserAct task status and prints timestamped progress messages.
Documents expected review fields such as author, rating, text, date, likes, and avatar URL.
limitations: Requires a BrowserAct API key and outbound network access.
Sends search terms and task requests to BrowserAct infrastructure.
Current script control flow may time out before retrieving final results.
Results depend on BrowserAct template availability and Google Maps data access.

use_cases.title: Local Business Review Collection
use_cases.description: Collect review data for a cafe, clinic, store, or venue in a selected country and language.

use_cases.title: Reputation Monitoring
use_cases.description: Track feedback for one brand location and review common praise, complaints, and rating patterns.

use_cases.title: Competitor Benchmarking
use_cases.description: Compare reviews for competing businesses in a city before planning outreach or market research.

prompt_templates.title: Find Reviews for One Business
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for one place or category.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to collect Google Maps reviews for "coffee shop in Austin" in English for the United States.

prompt_templates.title: Monitor a Specific Location
prompt_templates.scenario: Focused review tracking for one brand location.
prompt_templates.prompt: Collect recent Google Maps reviews for "Acme Dental Chicago" using English and United States settings. Summarize rating themes after the run.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Local Competitors
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate research across several similar businesses.
prompt_templates.prompt: Run review collection for three searches: "vegan restaurant Portland", "plant based restaurant Portland", and "healthy lunch Portland". Compare common review themes.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Multi-Region Review Research
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow with parameters and error handling.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a review research plan for "urgent care clinic" across the United States, Canada, and the United Kingdom. Use the right country code for each run and stop if authorization fails.

output_examples.input: Reviews for "coffee shop in Austin" with English and United States settings.
output_examples.output: A progress log shows the task starting, running, and either finishing or returning a clear API error.

output_examples.input: Reviews for a specific dental clinic.
output: Reviewer names and ratings are expected when results are available.
Review text, dates, likes, and profile links are documented output fields.
Missing or invalid API keys are reported before any task is started.

best_practices: Set BROWSERACT_API_KEY in the environment before running the skill.
Use precise search terms with a clear city, brand, or business category.
Review BrowserAct and Google Maps terms before collecting or storing review data.
anti_patterns: Do not paste API keys into shared prompts, logs, or issue reports.
Do not use vague searches when a specific location or category is required.
Do not retry repeatedly after an invalid authorization error.

faq.question: Does this skill need an API key?
faq.answer: Yes. It requires BROWSERACT_API_KEY to start BrowserAct workflow tasks.

faq.question: Does it scrape Google Maps directly from my machine?
faq.answer: No. The script calls the BrowserAct API, which runs the configured workflow remotely.

faq.question: What inputs can I control?
faq.answer: You can provide search keywords, language, and country values.

faq.question: What review fields are documented?
faq.answer: The skill documents reviewer name, profile URL, rating, review text, date, likes, and avatar URL.

faq.question: Is this safe to publish?
faq.answer: It is publishable with a warning because it executes a script and sends data to BrowserAct.

faq.question: What should I do if authorization fails?
faq.answer: Stop retrying and verify that the BrowserAct API key is present, current, and correct.
